title: Inject absolute tyre-compound and rule context from official F1/Pirelli sources
summary: Current features treat `SOFT/MEDIUM/HARD` as globally comparable, but F1 uses race-specific absolute compounds (`C1`-`C5/C6`), so the same `SOFT` can mean very different durability and degradation.
rationale: Current features treat `SOFT/MEDIUM/HARD` as globally comparable, but F1 uses race-specific absolute compounds (`C1`-`C5/C6`), so the same `SOFT` can mean very different durability and degradation. Official dry-race rules also create different baseline stop incentives than wet races, and Monaco 2025 is a special two-stop regime.
implementation_hint: Build a small external lookup keyed by `Year+Race` for actual hard/medium/soft compound nominations, sprint vs standard weekend, and special mandatory-stop rules. Add features like absolute compound ordinal, softness gap within the nominated trio, `current_is_softest_of_weekend`, and `mandatory_second_compound_still_unmet` for dry races.
expected_effect: Should improve race-to-race transfer and reduce the model’s reliance on crude fixed tyre-life thresholds.
risk: External mapping errors or incomplete race metadata can silently hurt CV; keep the first test to low-cardinality rule/compound features only.
